request.question: Who was the director of the movie Home which was an adaptation of the book The true Meaning of Smekday?

title: True meaning of Christmas
Home (2015 film)
Home (soundtrack)
Órlaith
Eckhart (TV series)
Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls
Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh
Baby Looney Tunes' Eggs-traordinary Adventure
The True Meaning of Smekday
The Ragamuffin Gospel
sentences: The "true meaning of Christmas" is a phrase with a long history in American pop culture.
It first appears in the mid-19th century, and is often given vaguely religious overtones, suggesting that the "true meaning of Christmas" is the celebration of the Nativity of Christ.

But in pop culture usage, overt religious references are mostly avoided, and the "true meaning" is taken to be a sort of introspective and benevolent attitude as opposed to the commercialization of Christmas which has been lamented since at least the 1850s.
The poem "A Visit From St. Nicholas" (1822) helped popularize the tradition of exchanging gifts, and seasonal Christmas shopping began to assume economic importance.
Harriet Beecher Stowe criticizes the commercialization of Christmas in her story "Christmas; or, the Good Fairy".
An early expression of this sentiment using the phrase of "the true meaning" is found in "The American magazine", vol.
Home is a 2015 American 3D computer-animated science fiction comedy-adventure film produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by 20th Century Fox.
It is loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book "The True Meaning of Smekday" and starring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin, Jennifer Lopez, and Matt Jones.
Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.
The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.
However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.
Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.
It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.
It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.
Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.
As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.
Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.
Órlaith (older spelling: Órfhlaith, newer spelling: Órla) is an Irish language female given name.
Orlagh is a hybrid spelling based on a merger with another suffix.

The meaning of the name dervies from Ór, meaning "Golden" and Flaith, meaning "prince" although as names with "flaith" suffixes are almost always exclusively feminine, this is usually interpreted as meaning "princess".
The retention of the 'fh' within the spelling maintains the ability to easily derive the true meaning of the name, but as this is silent, it is common to see this redacted.
All spellings of the name are however pronounced the same, as "OR-la".
Eckhart is a Canadian animated series presented on Teletoon Canada in 2000 and 2001.
It was created by David Weale and incorporates some of the sounds and culture of Weale's home province of Prince Edward Island in Canada.
Eckhart, the title character, was a mouse who was a character in a children's book by David Weale titled "The True Meaning of Crumbfest", which was also a Christmas special program on television.
Approximately 39 episodes of the program were produced.
It also was packaged for sale as a retail DVD product (26 episodes + the Christmas Special).
The program was broadcast in 25 countries around the world.
Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls is a book was written by Lawrence Schiffman, published in 1994 by Doubleday, as part of the Anchor Research Library.
The book's aim was to explain the true meaning of the Dead Sea Scrolls for Judaism and Christianity.
Previous to the publication of the book, many exaggerated and irresponsible claims about the scrolls were published.
"Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls" "sets before the public the real Dead Sea Scrolls."
Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.
It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.
Baby Looney Tunes' Eggs-traordinary Adventure is a 2003 American direct-to-video animated comedy film from Warner Bros.
Animation.
It is the first, and only, movie based on the TV series "Baby Looney Tunes".
The film follows the main characters from "Baby Looney Tunes" as they go on a search for the true meaning of Easter.
The True Meaning of Smekday is a 2007 children's book by Adam Rex that was highly recommended by "The New York Times".
The book was adapted into the 2015 animated feature film "Home".
The Ragamuffin Gospel is a book about the essence of Christianity by former Franciscan priest Brennan Manning.
Manning argues that Jesus' gospel was one of grace, and that efforts to earn salvation are impossibly misguided.

He states that the true meaning of God's grace has been lost in society amidst a constant search to merely please God, as though the Almighty is only a "small minded book keeper," who tallies sins and uses them against humanity.
Citing numerous biblical references and utilizing colleagues' stories, Manning illustrates the simple need for humanity to accept the freedom of God's grace, and its power to change lives.
A popular quote from the book: “To evangelize a person is to say to him or her: you, too, are loved by God in the Lord Jesus.”
It was first published in 1990.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Who was the director of the movie Home which was an adaptation of the book The true Meaning of Smekday?
hop1_docs: The True Meaning of Smekday | The True Meaning of Smekday is a 2007 children's book by Adam Rex that was highly recommended by "The New York Times".  The book was adapted into the 2015 animated feature film "Home".

Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh | Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.  It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.

Home (2015 film) | Home is a 2015 American 3D computer-animated science fiction comedy-adventure film produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by 20th Century Fox.  It is loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book "The True Meaning of Smekday" and starring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin, Jennifer Lopez, and Matt Jones.  Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.  The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.  However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.

Home (soundtrack) | Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.  It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.  It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.  Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.  As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.  Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.

If Only It Were True | If Only It Were True (original title: Et si c'etait vrai...) is French author Marc Levy's first novel, released in 1999.  It was translated into English in 2000 and was adapted into the American film "Just Like Heaven" in 2005.  A 2006 Bollywood movie named "I See You" is also an adaptation of this book.  2004 Malayalam movie "Vismayathumbathu" was also based on this book.

Marshall Pugh | Marshall Pugh (born 1925) is a British journalist and author.  He wrote a book called "Commander Crabb" based on the true story of a British officer who learned deep sea diving to thwart Italian frogmen who were sabotaging British naval forces.  He later adapted it into a movie called "The Silent Enemy" which was released in 1958.

Ömer Lütfi Akad | Ömer Lütfi Akad (2 September 1916 – 19 November 2011), was a Turkish film director, who directed movies from 1948 to 1974.  In 1949, he debuted as a film director with "Vurun Kahpeye" ("Strike the Whore") an adaptation of Halide Edip Adıvar's book of the same title.  He became one of the pioneers of the period in the "Director Generation".  The 1970s trilogy, "The Bride; The Wedding; and The Sacrifice", is considered his masterpiece.  Afterwards, he withdrew from movie making instead directing adaptations for TV.
response.summary_1: Passage 1 states that The True Meaning of Smekday "was adapted into the ... film 'Home'." Passage 3 (the "Home" film entry) explicitly says "Tim Johnson is the director of the film." Bridge: the film "Home" (the adaptation of the book) -> verify the "Home" film page (passage 3) for the director "Tim Johnson" using the quoted anchor "Tim Johnson is the director of the film."
response.hop2_query: Home film director Tim Johnson The True Meaning of Smekday adaptation

Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.  The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.  However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.

Tim Johnson (film director) | Tim Johnson (born August 27, 1961) is an American animation director.  Johnson has directed in many films such as "Antz", "" and "Over the Hedge".  Recently, he directed the DreamWorks 3D film "Home."

Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh | Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.  It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.

Home (soundtrack) | Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.  It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.  It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.  Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.  As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.  Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.

Caroline Thompson | Caroline Thompson (born April 23, 1956) is an American novelist, screenwriter, film director, and producer.  She wrote the screenplays for Tim Burton's films "Edward Scissorhands", "The Nightmare Before Christmas", and "Corpse Bride".  She co-wrote the story for "Edward Scissorhands" and recently co-adapted a new stage version of the film with director and choreographer Matthew Bourne.  Thompson also adapted the screenplay for the film version of "Wicked Lovely", a bestselling fantasy series, in 2011, but the production was put into turnaround.

Butterbox Babies (film) | Butterbox Babies is a film by Sullivan Entertainment and adapted from the book "Butterbox Babies" by Bette L. Cahill.  The film and book are based on the true story of the Ideal Maternity Home, a home for unwed pregnant mothers, during the Great Depression and Second World War.  The home made millions from the illegal adoption of illegitimate babies during the 1930s and 1940s.
response.summary_2: The 2015 film Home — the adaptation of Adam Rex's The True Meaning of Smekday — was directed by Tim Johnson.
